perturbation.original_text: 2. GRANT OF RIGHTS Subject to the terms and conditions and in consideration of the payments set forth herein and in the Primary Agreement, CSA as disclosed agent for Talent grants to Company from the Commencement Date the right and license during the Term of this Agreement in the Territory to use the "Property" in connection with the advertisement, promotion, and sale of the Property Training Course and the Product as well as the right to use such Property on the Product and related packaging. The above license grant shall apply to all material objects of the Property, in which the Property is fixed by any method now known or later developed, and from which the Property can be perceived, reproduced, or otherwise communicated, either directly or with the aid of a machine or device. The parties further understand and agree that Company hereby reserves the right to display its copyright notice on advertisements, promotions or other materials as well as all Product, no matter what form or media, containing the Property subject always to all and any copyright or other interests of the Talent. CSA on behalf of Talent further grants and assigns to Company the right to enforce applicable copyright and intellectual property laws against third party infringers or malfeasors on Talent's behalf or in Talent's name.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. GRANT OF RIGHTS Subject to the terms and conditions and in consideration of the payments set forth herein and in the Primary Agreement, CSA as disclosed agent for Talent grants to Company from the Commencement Date the right and license during the Term of this Agreement in the Territory to use the "Property" in connection with the advertisement, promotion, and sale of the Property Training Course and the Product as well as the right to use such Property on the Product and related packaging. The above license grant shall only apply to digital formats of the Property, in which the Property is fixed by any method now known or later developed, and from which the Property can be perceived, reproduced, or otherwise communicated, either directly or with the aid of a machine or device. The parties further understand and agree that Company hereby reserves the right to display its copyright notice on advertisements, promotions or other materials as well as all Product, no matter what form or media, containing the Property subject always to all and any copyright or other interests of the Talent. CSA on behalf of Talent further grants and assigns to Company the right to enforce applicable copyright and intellectual property laws against third party infringers or malfeasors on Talent's behalf or in Talent's name.
perturbation.explanation: The change limits the 'GRANT OF RIGHTS' in section 2, restricting the license grant to only 'digital formats' of the Property. This contradicts the broader permissions initially given to the Company to use the Property across 'all material objects' in the same section. The earlier language suggested unlimited usage. This in-text contradiction creates uncertainty about what the agreement covers.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. GRANT OF RIGHTS Subject to the terms and conditions and in consideration of the payments set forth herein and in the Primary Agreement, CSA as disclosed agent for Talent grants to Company from the Commencement Date the right and license during the Term of this Agreement in the Territory to use the "Property" in connection with the advertisement, promotion, and sale of the Property Training Course and the Product as well as the right to use such Property on the Product and related packaging. The above license grant shall only apply to digital formats of the Property, in which the Property is fixed by any method now known or later developed, and from which the Property can be perceived, reproduced, or otherwise communicated, either directly or with the aid of a machine or device. The parties further understand and agree that Company hereby reserves the right to display its copyright notice on advertisements, promotions or other materials as well as all Product, no matter what form or media, containing the Property subject always to all and any copyright or other interests of the Talent. CSA on behalf of Talent further grants and assigns to Company the right to enforce applicable copyright and intellectual property laws against third party infringers or malfeasors on Talent's behalf or in Talent's name.
perturbation.justification: The original text grants rights to use the 'Property' in connection with the advertisement, promotion, and sale of the Property Training Course and the Product as well as the right to use such Property on the Product and related packaging. It then states that 'The above license grant shall apply to all material objects of the Property'. The changed text limits this grant to only 'digital formats of the Property.' This directly contradicts the prior statement and the contradicted text that permits use on all material objects, thus creating a clear in-text contradiction within the section.

perturbation.original_text: 3. TERM This Agreement shall be effective as of the date of execution by both parties. The period of license granted shall be for the Term and shall extend for a period of twenty four (24) months or until terminated as per clause 8 herein. It is agreed that the Term may be extended upon mutual agreement between the parties.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. TERM This Agreement shall be effective as of the date of execution by both parties. The period of license granted shall be for the Term and shall extend for a period of twenty four (24) months, regardless of termination clause 8 herein. It is agreed that the Term may be extended upon mutual agreement between the parties.
perturbation.explanation: The modification in Section 3 changes the duration of the agreement. The original states the term extends 24 months or until terminated as per clause 8. The modified states that clause 8 will have no affect on the term, it will extend for 24 months, regardless of the termination. This in-text contradiction creates uncertainty and would cause disputes on if the agreement can be terminated prior to the 24 month term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. TERM This Agreement shall be effective as of the date of execution by both parties. The period of license granted shall be for the Term and shall extend for a period of twenty four (24) months, regardless of termination clause 8 herein. It is agreed that the Term may be extended upon mutual agreement between the parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 3 directly contradicts the original intention of allowing termination as per clause 8. The original text states the agreement extends for 24 months 'or until terminated as per clause 8', while the modified text removes this contingency, stating the term will extend for 24 months 'regardless of termination clause 8 herein'. This creates a direct conflict and uncertainty as to whether the agreement can be terminated before the 24-month term, fulfilling the definition of a structural flaw leading to an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 18. SURVIVAL The parties' rights and obligations under the Primary Agreement and Articles 4, 6, 7, 9, 10, 12, 13, 14, and 15 shall survive any expiration or earlier termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 18. SURVIVAL The parties' rights and obligations under the Primary Agreement and Articles 4, 6, 7, 9, 10, 12, 13, 14, and 15 shall not survive any expiration or earlier termination of this Agreement, only termination due to a breach in the agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Section 18 initially states the the rights and obligation under the Primary Agreement, survive and expiration of earlier termination. However, the changed text states that only termination due to breach is the only termination type that the Primary Agreements survives through. This leads to confusion on if the Primary Agreements rights and obligation are still valid if there is not a breach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 18. SURVIVAL The parties' rights and obligations under the Primary Agreement and Articles 4, 6, 7, 9, 10, 12, 13, 14, and 15 shall not survive any expiration or earlier termination of this Agreement, only termination due to a breach in the agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that rights and obligations survive any expiration or earlier termination. The changed text limits this survival to only terminations due to breach. This directly contradicts the original broad survival clause, creating a clear inconsistency about which termination types allow for the continuation of rights and obligations, thus falling under the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.
